reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 3 (Code Verification) - PASSED: Code reading is accurate. GasLimitTxFilter:19-38 uses Math.Min(blockGasLimit, configuredGasLimit) without incorporating EIP-7825 cap (16777216). The EIP-7825 cap is only enforced by GasLimitCapTxValidator (TxValidator.cs:380-391), which runs during HeadTxValidator maintenance (TxPool.cs:724) triggered on head changes, not at transaction entry. Gate 4 (Exploitability) - PASSED: Attacker can directly submit transactions with gasLimit between 16777216 and blockGasLimit via P2P or RPC. Attack path is attacker-triggered. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference) - PASSED: 01e property PROP-aa9e39fd-inv-009 (outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W1B2_1771748568.json:172-189) requires 'forall tx, B: tx.gasLimit > 16777216 => reject(tx) regardless of B.gasLimit'. Code deviates by allowing txpool entry when blockGasLimit permits. Severity downgrade: Impact is limited to mempool resource exhaustion until next head change (typically 12 seconds). Transactions cannot enter blocks due to TxFilterPipeline in block production (TxFilterPipelineBuilder.cs:19 adds HeadTxValidator). Network impact < 0.01% (single-client mempool pollution). An attacker can trigger this via P2P by submitting transactions with gasLimit=20000000 when blockGasLimit=30000000, causing temporary mempool slot occupation and peer broadcast until head change triggers eviction.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Gas cap applies regardless of block gas limit set by validators. (assertion: forall tx, B: tx.gasLimit > 16777216 => reject(tx) regardless of B.gasLimit)
